props.title: We’ll be back for State of the Browser 2025
props.description: We’ll be returning to State of the Browser in London at the end of March.  We’ll be around all day to talk about UI and branding. We’d love to see you there.

props.title: We’ll be back for State of the Browser 2025
props.author: Paul Love
props.content:
We’ll be going along to State of the Browser 2025. We had such a nice time at the [last one](https://measured.co/blog/catch-up-at-state-of-the-browser) we thought we’d go again.

## Essential details

- Saturday, 29 March, 2024
- 8:30 am–3:00 pm
- The Barbican Centre, Frobisher Auditorium 1 ([Map](https://www.google.co.uk/maps/place/Barbican+Centre/@51.5209863,-0.0957614,17z/data=!4m6!3m5!1s0x48761b56fb64b275:0xc756e26675d21f40!8m2!3d51.5202077!4d-0.0937864!16zL20vMG02cTY?entry=ttu)) — We recommend entering by Silk Street
- [Ticket information](https://2025.stateofthebrowser.com/tickets/)

## About the event

State of the Browser is a super event for anyone who works in the world of web. The talks are always engaging, and it’s a really lovely vibe in person (though you can attend online too).

This year’s speaker lineup is shaping up nicely. You can follow speaker announcements on London Web Standards’ social channels: [Bluesky](https://bsky.app/profile/londonwebstandards.org), [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/londonwebstandards/), and [Mastodon](https://mastodon.world/@webstandards).

## Find us on the day

We won’t be speaking, but will be around all day to talk about UI, branding and accessibility — or whatever else grabs you. (Weather chat is still most welcome.)

Once more, thanks to London Web Standards for letting us sponsor. We‘re pleased to be involved in a small way.

Here’s the [ticket link](https://2024.stateofthebrowser.com/tickets/) once more.
